summary: ReactiveUI testing guidance for creating new test projects and upgrading legacy test projects to the latest ReactiveUI.Testing patterns.
recommendedPatterns: Use ReactiveUI.Testing for scheduler-aware tests, command execution verification, and observable behavior assertions.
Create new ReactiveUI test projects with explicit scheduler control and avoid wall-clock sleeps.
Migrate legacy test projects by replacing brittle time-based tests with ReactiveUI.Testing or Rx scheduler-driven tests.
avoidPatterns: Do not rely on wall-clock sleeps in reactive tests when scheduler control is available.
Do not leave legacy tests coupled to implicit global scheduler state.
Do not upgrade application packages without aligning the corresponding ReactiveUI.Testing package usage in tests.
setupSteps: Add ReactiveUI.Testing to test projects that exercise reactive behavior.
Use test schedulers or ReactiveUI.Testing helpers for timing-sensitive logic.
For migration work, replace brittle timing or global-state tests incrementally and rerun the suite frequently.
commonPitfalls: Failing to reset test-specific global state or scheduler assumptions.
Leaving old test helper patterns in place after package upgrades.

sources.title: ReactiveUI repository
sources.notes: ReactiveUI testing package and scheduler-aware testing guidance.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated from local ReactiveUI repositories, official GitHub documentation, and current SDK/package guidance.
harvest.notes: Prepared for the ReactiveUI MCP Server knowledge catalog.
